perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW only.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting the amendment's application to only PCQ and ABW and excluding their subsidiaries and related companies, the contract potentially avoids extending consumer protections or liabilities to these related entities. This could become an issue, especially if these subsidiaries are involved in activities directly related to the Co-Branding Agreement, leading to legal risks if those subsidiaries operate in jurisdictions with specific consumer protection laws that would have applied if included in the original agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: The California Consumer Legal Remedies Act (CLRA) aims to protect consumers against unfair or deceptive practices. If subsidiaries and related companies of PCQ and ABW are involved in activities that could potentially violate the CLRA, excluding them from the amendment's application could be interpreted as an attempt to shield them from liability under this law. By not including the subsidiaries, the contract now has the potential to skirt around the consumer protection requirements of California's CLRA, especially regarding false advertising or misrepresentation, if the subsidiaries engage in such behavior related to the co-branding agreement.

OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 4. OBLIGATIONS ARISING FROM PARTICULAR TRANSACTIONS [1738 - 3273.69] ( Part 4 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 1.5. CONSUMERS LEGAL REMEDIES ACT [1750 - 1784] ( Title 1.5 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1550. ) CHAPTER 1. General Provisions [1750 - 1756] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1550. ) 1750. This title may be cited as the Consumers Legal Remedies Act. (Added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1550.)
perturbation.justification: The original contract language covers PCQ and ABW, including their subsidiaries and related companies. The changed text limits the amendment's application to only PCQ and ABW, excluding the subsidiaries. The Law Explanation states that excluding these subsidiaries could be an attempt to shield them from liability under the California Consumer Legal Remedies Act (CLRA), particularly if they engage in activities related to false advertising or misrepresentation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 identifies California Civil Code section 1750, which is the beginning of the Consumers Legal Remedies Act. By excluding the subsidiaries, the changed text creates a loophole that may shield them from consumer protection requirements under the CLRA, contradicting the intent of the law.
